message.content:
You are working on branch str-1o4m which has proptest additions for `shatter-core/src/input_gen.rs` and `shatter-core/src/shrink.rs`. The branch can't merge cleanly because main has diverged.

Your task: Apply the useful remaining work from this branch onto main. Specifically:

1. Create a new branch from main
2. The `input_gen.rs` proptest additions on str-1o4m likely overlap with what's already on main. Check what main already has and only add what's missing.
3. The `shrink.rs` proptest additions (177 lines of property tests in a `mod prop_tests` block) are net-new. Apply those.
4. Make sure `cargo test -p shatter-core` passes
5. Make sure `cargo clippy -p shatter-core -- -D warnings` passes
6. Commit the result

Here's what the branch adds to `shrink.rs` — a new `mod prop_tests` block at the end of the `mod tests` block with proptest properties for `shrink_candidates`: never contains original (int/float/str), type preservation (int/float/str), int abs <= original, string length <= original, array length <= original, no duplicates, plus unit tests for minimal cases (zero, empty string, false, null, empty array).

For `input_gen.rs`, the branch adds: a `value_matches_type` helper, `mutate_inputs_preserves_types_arbitrary`, `mutate_inputs_actually_mutates`, `crossover_inputs_preserves_length_arbitrary`, and `crossover_inputs_type_compatible` proptests.

Check what already exists on main in both files before adding anything.

content.text: I'll start by examining what's currently on main and what's on the branch.

input.command: cd /home/ketan/project/shatter && git log --oneline -5 str-1o4m 2>/dev/null || echo "Branch not found locally"

content.content:
dc25687 test: add proptest coverage for input_gen mutation/crossover and shrink
c7640c2 Merge branch 'str-bmh'
6d08a15 Merge branch 'str-qx1m'
3bc8ab1 test: add proptest coverage for merge_file_spec_bundles
cdfee50 fix(shatter-ts): exclude __fixtures__ from tsc build

content.text: Now let me look at what the branch adds vs what main already has.
Now let me check what main already has in these files.

input.description: Check end of shrink.rs on main

content.text: The shrink.rs prop_tests are already on main. Let me verify more precisely.

input.description: Check if prop_tests already exist in shrink.rs on main

input.command: cd /home/ketan/project/shatter && git show main:shatter-core/src/shrink.rs | grep "fn shrink_\|mod prop_tests"
input.description: List all test functions in shrink.rs on main

content.text: The shrink.rs prop_tests are already fully on main. Now let me check input_gen.rs.

input.command: cd /home/ketan/project/shatter && git show main:shatter-core/src/input_gen.rs | grep "fn mutate_inputs_preserves_types_arbitrary\|fn mutate_inputs_actually_mutates\|fn crossover_inputs_preserves_length_arbitrary\|fn crossover_inputs_type_compatible\|fn value_matches_type"
input.description: Check if branch's input_gen tests already exist on main

content.text:
All the changes from the str-1o4m branch are already present on main. Both the shrink.rs prop_tests and the input_gen.rs additions (value_matches_type helper, mutate_inputs_preserves_types_arbitrary, mutate_inputs_actually_mutates, crossover_inputs_preserves_length_arbitrary, crossover_inputs_type_compatible) already exist on main.

There is nothing new to cherry-pick — all the proptest additions from the str-1o4m branch have already been merged into main.
